prerequisites: No account, API key, or credentials are required. The xkcd JSON interface is publicly accessible over HTTPS.
Network access to xkcd.com so the connector can reach the JSON endpoints (for example https://xkcd.com/info.0.json for the current comic).
Optionally, the number of a specific comic to sync, supplied through the comic_number config field.

authMethods.name: None (public API)
authMethods.summary: The xkcd JSON interface requires no authentication. Endpoints such as https://xkcd.com/info.0.json (current comic) and https://xkcd.com/614/info.0.json (a specific comic) are fetched directly over HTTPS with no API key or token. The connector descriptor accordingly defines no secret fields.

setupSteps.title: Confirm access to the xkcd JSON interface
setupSteps.body: The xkcd JSON interface exposes comic metadata at URLs of the form https://xkcd.com/info.0.json for the current comic and https://xkcd.com/<number>/info.0.json for a specific comic. These return plaintext JSON with the comic title, URLs, post date, transcript when available, and other metadata. Verify the host is reachable from where pm runs; no credentials are needed.

setupSteps.title: Optionally set the comic number
setupSteps.body: Leave comic_number empty to pull data relative to the current comic, or set comic_number to a specific comic's number (for example 614) to target https://xkcd.com/<number>/info.0.json. This is the only configuration field for this connector and it is optional.

setupSteps.title: Run the connector
setupSteps.body: Because no secrets are required, no credential configuration is needed. Provide the optional comic_number if desired and run the source to fetch comic metadata as JSON.
